project.name: ruvector slice 5a — ruvllm (LLM inference engine)
project.description: LLM serving runtime with Ruvector integration - Paged attention, KV cache, and SONA learning Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: .reasoning_bank_patterns
nodes.summary: Persisted reasoning-bank pattern store (~1.5MB JSON blob) consumed by the ruvllm reasoning_bank subsystem to seed pattern retrieval at startup for the LLM inference engine.

nodes.name: moe_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark suite for ruvllm's Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) infrastructure, covering router overhead, expert cache hit rates, eviction policies, prefetching, SIMD affinity decay, and batch scheduling end-to-end.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses criterion::black_box and BenchmarkGroup to isolate hot paths in MoE serving.

nodes.name: benchmark_model.rs
nodes.summary: CLI example that loads a ruvllm model (real or simulated), runs token-generation benchmarks, and emits text or JSON reports with tokens/s, percentile latencies, and peak-memory metrics for the LLM serving engine.

nodes.name: coreml_backend.rs
nodes.summary: Apple CoreML/ANE inference backend for ruvllm — wraps .mlmodel/.mlpackage loading, ANE capability detection, tokenizer integration, streaming and non-streaming token generation, and compute-unit selection (CPU/GPU/ANE) for the LLM engine on Apple Silicon.
nodes.languageNotes: Conditionally compiled with #[cfg(target_os = "macos")] and falls back to a stub CoreMLBackend on non-macOS targets (the second class definition at L1609).

nodes.name: expert_cache.rs
nodes.summary: Hot/cold expert weight cache for BitNet MoE inference in ruvllm — implements LRU/LFU/adaptive eviction with affinity-aware scoring, prefetch hints, and a MoE batch scheduler to keep frequently routed experts resident in fast memory.

nodes.name: tl1_wasm.rs
nodes.summary: WASM-SIMD (v128) implementation of BitNet TL1 GEMV — packed ternary weight unpacking, int16 activation quantization, and horizontal sum reductions for in-browser LLM inference fallback.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses core::arch::wasm32 intrinsics (i16x8 ops) gated on target_arch = "wasm32" with a scalar fallback.

nodes.name: model_router.rs
nodes.summary: Three-tier (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) Claude model router for ruvllm — analyzes prompt complexity across token, reasoning, domain, security, planning, and performance axes, then selects a model with cost/latency criteria and learns from feedback to calibrate routing accuracy.

nodes.name: semantic_cache.rs
nodes.summary: Semantic tool-result cache that deduplicates LLM tool calls via embedding similarity — supports exact and approximate lookup, TTL expiration, per-tool invalidation, and stats reporting to cut redundant tool-call latency in the ruvllm serving loop.

nodes.name: report.rs
nodes.summary: Evaluation reporting layer for ruvllm — aggregates correctness, economics, and harness results into markdown/JSON leaderboards and ablation comparisons for benchmarking inference quality vs cost.

nodes.name: model_card.rs
nodes.summary: HuggingFace-style model card data model and markdown serializer for ruvllm-published artifacts — captures task, framework, license, parameters, datasets, metrics, and tags with a fluent ModelCardBuilder.

nodes.name: kernels/mod.rs
nodes.summary: Compute-kernel umbrella module for ruvllm — re-exports Accelerate/NEON/ANE/attention/matmul/norm/quantized/rope kernels and exposes runtime capability detection plus an AttentionConfig used by the LLM forward pass.

nodes.name: micro_lora.rs
nodes.summary: MicroLoRA online-adaptation engine for ruvllm — low-rank adapters with NEON-SIMD forward, gradient accumulation, EWC-regularized updates, target-module selection, and serializable state for runtime fine-tuning of the LLM.
nodes.languageNotes: Heavy use of #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")] NEON intrinsics; forward_simd_neon_impl is a 250-line vectorized hot path.

nodes.name: attention_fused.metal
nodes.summary: Metal compute shader implementing fused Flash Attention 2 (Q*K^T -> softmax -> *V) for Apple Silicon M4 Pro with online softmax, GQA support, causal masking, and tiled shared-memory loads to power ruvllm's GPU attention path.
nodes.languageNotes: MSL with simdgroup_matrix and vectorized half4 access; constants tuned for M4 Pro (1024 threads/threadgroup, 64-tile).

nodes.name: ruvltra_medium.rs
nodes.summary: RuvLtra-Medium model definition — decoder-only transformer with GQA attention, RoPE, optional Sona hooks for live adaptation, paged-KV-cache and speculative-decoding integration, and base/coder/agent variant presets for the ruvllm inference stack.
nodes.languageNotes: matmul_neon and flash_attention paths are #[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")] hot kernels.

nodes.name: sona_llm.rs
nodes.summary: Sona-LLM continual learning loop that combines MicroLoRA instant adaptation, background consolidation, and deep optimization phases with multiple consolidation strategies (EWC, ensemble, quality-weighted, best) to evolve the ruvllm model online without catastrophic forgetting.

nodes.name: ste.rs
nodes.summary: Straight-Through Estimator (STE) gradient routines for Quantization-Aware Training — backward passes that propagate gradients through non-differentiable quantization ops and compute scale gradients for ruvllm's QAT pipeline.

nodes.name: quantize/mod.rs
nodes.summary: Quantization umbrella module that re-exports Hadamard, incoherence, PI-Quant (scalar+SIMD), QuIP, RuvLtra-quant, TurboQuant (and profile), and security helpers used to compress ruvllm model weights for fast/cheap inference.

nodes.name: reasoning_bank/mod.rs
nodes.summary: ReasoningBank facade that stores trajectories, distills patterns from verdicts, consolidates and prunes low-quality entries, and supports import/export — feeding learned reasoning patterns back into ruvllm prompt routing and model adaptation.

nodes.name: serving/batch.rs
nodes.summary: Continuous-batching primitives for the ruvllm serving loop — splits requests into prefill/decode tasks, plans iterations against a token budget, merges prefill+decode batches, and collects KV-slot/position/input-id tensors for the model forward pass.

nodes.name: ruvltra_pretrain.rs
nodes.summary: RuvLtra pretraining harness that seeds the Sona engine and ReasoningBank with routing patterns, quality-prediction signals, and reasoning categories — provides ready-made configs for ruvltra-small and edge deployment scenarios.

nodes.name: claude_dataset.rs
nodes.summary: Claude-task synthetic dataset generator and curator for ruvllm training — categorized templates (coder, researcher, security, architecture, reviewer), complexity/domain metadata, augmentation (paraphrase, complexity variation, cross-domain transfer), and JSON/JSONL export with train/eval split.

nodes.name: acceptance_gates.rs
nodes.summary: Acceptance-gate integration tests that pin ruvllm release-quality thresholds (latency, throughput, correctness, memory) and act as CI checkpoints before shipping a new LLM inference build.

nodes.name: tests/fixtures/mod.rs
nodes.summary: Shared test-fixture helpers for ruvllm integration tests — synthesizes long/repetition/numeric prompts and provides coherence, n-gram repetition, consecutive-repeat, and alphanumeric-ratio analyzers used by acceptance and MoE tests.

nodes.name: moe_integration.rs
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration tests for ruvllm's MoE stack — exercises routing, expert-cache promotion/eviction, prefetch, and batched scheduling against realistic token streams to validate the engine's MoE serving correctness.

nodes.name: BenchmarkResults
nodes.summary: Aggregated benchmark output — captures throughput, latencies, and percentile/mean/median/std statistics derived from a batch of GenerationResult entries.

nodes.name: CoreMLBackend
nodes.summary: Top-level CoreML inference backend — owns the loaded mlmodel handle, tokenizer, compute units, and exposes generate / generate_stream / get_embeddings for the ruvllm engine on Apple platforms.

nodes.name: AneCapabilities
nodes.summary: Runtime probe describing Apple Neural Engine availability and version used to gate ANE-targeted code paths.

nodes.name: generate_stream_v2
nodes.summary: Updated streaming-generation entry point that yields tokens incrementally from a CoreML model with proper EOS handling and KV-state propagation.

nodes.name: load_model
nodes.summary: Loads a .mlmodel/.mlpackage from disk, validates its path, attaches the tokenizer, and configures compute-unit policy for inference.

nodes.name: ExpertCache
nodes.summary: Core hot/cold expert cache used by BitNet MoE serving — tracks access stats, applies eviction policies, and exposes affinity-aware admit/evict/prefetch APIs.

nodes.name: MoeBatchScheduler
nodes.summary: Batch scheduler that groups MoE requests by expert affinity so cache residency is preserved across a serving iteration.

nodes.name: compute_base_eviction_scores
nodes.summary: Computes per-expert eviction-score baselines combining recency, frequency, and affinity signals for the adaptive eviction policy.

nodes.name: tl1_gemv_wasm
nodes.summary: WASM-SIMD packed-ternary GEMV kernel — unpacks ternary indices, multiplies against quantized int16 activations, and accumulates with i16x8 horizontal sums.

nodes.name: TaskComplexityAnalyzer
nodes.summary: Analyzes a prompt across code/reasoning/security/planning/performance/domain axes to produce a calibrated ComplexityScore consumed by the model router.

nodes.name: ModelSelector
nodes.summary: Selects a Claude tier (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) given a ComplexityScore and SelectionCriteria, records outcomes for accuracy calibration, and produces ranked alternatives.

nodes.name: ModelRouter
nodes.summary: Top-level façade tying TaskComplexityAnalyzer and ModelSelector into a single route() entry point used by Claude-Flow callers.

nodes.name: analyze
nodes.summary: Primary complexity-analysis routine — combines all axis scores into the final calibrated ComplexityScore with confidence and recommended model.

nodes.name: apply_criteria
nodes.summary: Applies operator-supplied selection criteria (cost ceiling, latency budget, model allow/deny lists) to filter the model shortlist.

nodes.name: SemanticToolCache
nodes.summary: Embedding-keyed cache for LLM tool-call results with TTL, exact and approximate lookup, per-tool invalidation, and stats reporting.

nodes.name: get
nodes.summary: Approximate-match lookup that ranks cached tool results by embedding similarity and returns the best entry above the threshold.

nodes.name: store_with_options
nodes.summary: Stores a tool result with custom TTL/embedding/metadata options and enforces the cache size limit by evicting low-value entries.

nodes.name: to_markdown
nodes.summary: Renders a leaderboard-and-ablation report as Markdown for human review, with tables of correctness, economics, and harness scores.

nodes.name: ModelCard
nodes.summary: Canonical model-card record mirroring HuggingFace's schema — task, framework, license, parameters, datasets, metrics, tags, and arbitrary metadata.

nodes.name: ModelCardBuilder
nodes.summary: Fluent builder that assembles a ModelCard step-by-step with task/framework/license/dataset/metric setters and a terminal build().

nodes.name: to_markdown
nodes.summary: Serializes a ModelCard to HuggingFace-flavored Markdown with YAML frontmatter for hub publishing.

nodes.name: AttentionConfig
nodes.summary: Shared attention configuration (head count, head dim, scale, GQA group size, causal flag) consumed by the attention kernels across ruvllm models.

nodes.name: LoraAdapter
nodes.summary: Single low-rank adapter (A,B matrices) with NEON-SIMD forward, EWC-regularized gradient updates, and exportable state for ruvllm online fine-tuning.

nodes.name: MicroLoRA
nodes.summary: MicroLoRA collection that manages a set of LoraAdapters across target modules and orchestrates adapt() training on feedback samples.

nodes.name: forward_simd_neon_impl
nodes.summary: NEON-vectorized LoRA forward path — 250-line hot kernel using aarch64 intrinsics for low-latency adapter application on Apple Silicon.

nodes.name: apply_gradients_with_ewc
nodes.summary: Applies accumulated gradients with EWC penalty to prevent catastrophic forgetting during ruvllm online adaptation.

nodes.name: RuvLtraMediumConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for the RuvLtra-Medium transformer — hidden size, layers, heads, GQA group size, RoPE settings, Sona hooks, quant mode, and base/coder/agent presets.

nodes.name: RuvLtraMediumAttention
nodes.summary: GQA attention block with RoPE and an optional flash-attention path for the RuvLtra-Medium decoder.

nodes.name: RuvLtraMediumModel
nodes.summary: End-to-end RuvLtra-Medium decoder — stacks decoder layers, wires KV cache and Sona hooks, and exposes forward() + enable_sona_with_hooks().

nodes.name: base
nodes.summary: Builds the canonical RuvLtra-Medium base configuration with sensible dim/heads/layer defaults.

nodes.name: flash_attention
nodes.summary: Flash Attention 2 path used when the runtime detects an Apple Silicon GPU — calls into Metal kernels for the heavy lifting.

nodes.name: matmul_neon
nodes.summary: NEON-vectorized matmul micro-kernel for the linear projections inside the RuvLtra-Medium attention block.

nodes.name: SonaLlm
nodes.summary: Sona continual-learning controller — owns the MicroLoRA, training queue, and consolidation engine, and exposes instant_adapt / background_consolidate / deep_optimize.

nodes.name: instant_adapt
nodes.summary: Fast in-iteration adaptation — applies a MicroLoRA update from a feedback sample without blocking the serving loop.

nodes.name: background_consolidate
nodes.summary: Off-the-hot-path consolidation that merges queued training samples using the configured ConsolidationStrategy (EWC, ensemble, quality-weighted, best, average).

nodes.name: deep_optimize
nodes.summary: Periodic heavyweight optimization pass that retrains adapters end-to-end against the accumulated reasoning bank.

nodes.name: compute_scale_grad
nodes.summary: Computes the gradient of the quantization scale parameter via the straight-through estimator, used in QAT training of ruvllm models.

nodes.name: backward
nodes.summary: Per-element STE backward — clips gradients outside the quantization range and passes them through the identity inside it.

nodes.name: backward_batch
nodes.summary: Batched STE backward that vectorizes the per-element pass over a tensor slice.

nodes.name: ReasoningBank
nodes.summary: Top-level reasoning-bank — stores trajectories, distills patterns from verdicts, consolidates and prunes entries, and supports import/export to the .reasoning_bank_patterns store.

nodes.name: store_trajectory
nodes.summary: Persists a completed reasoning trajectory, deduplicating against existing patterns and updating quality stats.

nodes.name: distill
nodes.summary: Distills new reusable patterns from accumulated trajectories using clustering and verdict-weighted scoring.

nodes.name: ScheduledBatch
nodes.summary: A scheduled batch ready for the model forward pass — owns request lists and exposes helpers to collect input ids, positions, and KV slots.

nodes.name: IterationPlan
nodes.summary: Per-iteration plan describing prefill+decode work allocated against a TokenBudget for continuous batching.

nodes.name: TokenBudget
nodes.summary: Token-accounting helper that gates prefill/decode allocation per iteration so the engine doesn't exceed its per-step compute budget.

nodes.name: to_scheduled_batch
nodes.summary: Materializes an IterationPlan into a concrete ScheduledBatch ready to feed the model's forward pass.

nodes.name: RuvLtraPretrainer
nodes.summary: Pretraining orchestrator that warms the Sona engine and ReasoningBank with routing/quality samples and seeds canonical reasoning patterns.

nodes.name: pretrain_routing_patterns
nodes.summary: Trains the Sona engine on synthetic routing examples to bootstrap calibrated model-tier predictions before the first live request.

nodes.name: pretrain_quality_patterns
nodes.summary: Trains Sona's quality-prediction head on synthetic prompt/response pairs labeled with quality scores.

nodes.name: seed_reasoning_bank
nodes.summary: Seeds the ReasoningBank with default pattern categories so retrieval works from the first request.

nodes.name: ClaudeTaskDataset
nodes.summary: In-memory Claude-style task dataset with stats, train/eval split, and JSON/JSONL export for ruvllm training pipelines.

nodes.name: DatasetGenerator
nodes.summary: Generates synthetic Claude-task examples across coder/researcher/security/architecture/reviewer templates with complexity/domain augmentations.

nodes.name: coder_templates
nodes.summary: Returns the catalog of coder-task templates used to synthesize training prompts targeting the coder/agent variant of ruvllm models.

nodes.name: augment_examples
nodes.summary: Augments a base set of examples via paraphrasing, complexity variation, and cross-domain transfer to grow training coverage.

nodes.name: get_template_replacements
nodes.summary: Provides the slot-fill values used by the template instantiator (languages, tools, domains, etc.) when synthesizing examples.

nodes.name: run_real_benchmark
nodes.summary: Loads a real ruvllm model and runs the configured prompt set under the benchmark harness, capturing per-iteration latency and throughput.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point that parses args, dispatches to real or simulated benchmark, and emits the report in text or JSON.

nodes.name: bench_cache_hit_rate_simulation
nodes.summary: Simulates realistic MoE token streams and measures expert-cache hit-rate / latency across eviction-policy configurations.

nodes.name: bench_memory_aware_router
nodes.summary: Benchmarks the memory-aware MoE router under varying expert-cache footprints to validate it stays under the configured budget.

nodes.name: is_coherent
nodes.summary: Heuristic coherence check used by acceptance tests to flag degenerate generations (excessive repetition, alphanumeric ratio, etc.).

nodes.name: calculate_ngram_repetition
nodes.summary: Computes an n-gram repetition score over a generated sequence used as a soft quality gate in integration tests.

nodes.name: generate_long_prompt
nodes.summary: Synthesizes long-context prompts used to exercise the KV-cache and continuous-batching paths in integration tests.

nodes.name: CHANGELOG.md
nodes.summary: Release history for the ruvllm crate documenting v2.0.0 LLM-serving milestones: multi-threaded GEMM/GEMV, Flash Attention 2, quantized inference, paged attention, MicroLoRA, and Apple Metal kernels with M4 Pro performance benchmarks.

nodes.name: norm_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion microbenchmark suite for RMSNorm and LayerNorm kernels comparing NEON SIMD vs scalar implementations across LLM hidden-dim sizes, validating the sub-microsecond normalization targets the serving engine depends on.
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-written NEON intrinsics gated behind aarch64 cfg flags; criterion harness measures throughput across typical transformer dims (768-8192).

nodes.name: download_test_model.rs
nodes.summary: Example CLI that fetches GGUF test models from HuggingFace and ruvltra mirrors using curl/wget/rust-native fallbacks, providing reproducible model fixtures for ruvllm inference experiments.

nodes.name: gemma2.rs
nodes.summary: Native Rust implementation of the Gemma2 transformer (2B/9B/27B) with sliding-window attention, logit soft-capping, and RoPE positional encoding; serves as a first-class backend for the ruvllm serving engine.
nodes.languageNotes: NEON-optimized logit_soft_cap and matrix paths; weight loading mirrors the Gemma2 reference layout for direct GGUF compatibility.

nodes.name: gguf_export.rs
nodes.summary: Serializes BitNet T1.58 ternary models to GGUF format with custom metadata, half-precision conversion, and validation, enabling distribution of trained BitNet checkpoints to standard GGUF loaders.

nodes.name: tokenizer.rs
nodes.summary: Byte-level BPE tokenizer adapted to BitNet models with special-token support and round-trip byte/token encoding used by the BitNet inference path.

nodes.name: pretrain_pipeline.rs
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the four-phase Claude-Flow pretraining pipeline (bootstrap, synthetic, reinforce, consolidate) with curriculum scheduling, quality gates, and SONA-style pattern checkpointing for self-improving model training.
nodes.languageNotes: 13 supporting structs encode the full pipeline state machine; phase results feed back into the curriculum scheduler each iteration.

nodes.name: working_memory.rs
nodes.summary: Agent working-memory subsystem combining task context, scratchpad entries with embeddings, attention weights, and cached tool results to give the serving engine short-term recall between requests.

nodes.name: swe_bench.rs
nodes.summary: SWE-bench task loader and filtering harness for evaluating ruvllm-served models on real GitHub bug-fix benchmarks, with JSONL ingest, sampling, and statistics summarization.

nodes.name: progress.rs
nodes.summary: Terminal progress-bar primitives with ETA, speed formatting, and multi-bar coordination used by the model-download hub to surface fetch progress to users.

nodes.name: norm.rs
nodes.summary: Normalization kernels (RMSNorm and LayerNorm) with hand-written NEON SIMD paths, scalar fallbacks, and batched variants — the hot-path layer normalization used by every transformer forward in the serving engine.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `#[cfg(target_arch = "aarch64")]` to gate ARM NEON intrinsics; scalar versions cover x86_64 and WASM.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Module barrel for the LoRA subsystem, re-exporting adapter, micro-LoRA, and training APIs that enable per-request low-rank adaptation during serving.

nodes.name: fused_ops.metal
nodes.summary: Apple Metal compute shaders implementing fused transformer ops (fused matmul, RMSNorm, softmax, SwiGLU) tuned for M-series GPUs to accelerate the Metal backend of the ruvllm serving engine.
nodes.languageNotes: Metal Shading Language with simdgroup_matrix intrinsics for M4 Pro tensor throughput.

nodes.name: affinity.rs
nodes.summary: Mixture-of-Experts expert-affinity tracker with NEON/AVX2-accelerated score decay, top-k routing, and frequency percentile statistics — drives expert selection for MoE serving paths.

nodes.name: paged_attention.rs
nodes.summary: Paged attention implementation with block-allocated KV cache, page-table lookups, and free-list management — enables non-contiguous KV memory for high concurrent serving throughput.

nodes.name: training_loop.rs
nodes.summary: Quantization-Aware Training trainer with calibration, per-step metrics, epoch checkpoints, and resume support — produces low-precision weights consumable by the pi_quant pipeline.

nodes.name: pi_quant.rs
nodes.summary: Pi-quant 2-bit and 3-bit weight quantization with scalar, NEON, and AVX2 paths plus packed block layouts and MSE/spectral/cosine quality metrics — the foundational compression layer used by ruvllm's quantized inference.
nodes.languageNotes: 1867 lines split into encoder, packer/unpacker, SIMD kernels, and quality-metric helpers; runtime CPU dispatch picks the fastest available path.

nodes.name: pattern_store.rs
nodes.summary: Reasoning-bank pattern store that ingests trajectories and lessons, classifies them by category, supports cosine-similarity retrieval, and prunes low-quality patterns — feeds learned reasoning back into the serving engine.

nodes.name: engine.rs
nodes.summary: Central ruvllm serving engine that submits, schedules, generates, streams, and times out inference requests; orchestrates the scheduler, KV cache manager, speculative decoder, and real-time optimizer for the LLM-serving entry point.
nodes.languageNotes: ServingEngine owns the model, draft model, scheduler, and async run loops; submit/stream/run_async expose blocking and Tokio-based APIs side by side.

nodes.name: speculative.rs
nodes.summary: Speculative decoding implementation with draft/verify phases, tree-of-thoughts speculation, adaptive lookahead, atomic acceptance statistics, and NEON-optimized softmax/sampling helpers used by the serving engine for throughput speedups.

nodes.name: contrastive.rs
nodes.summary: Contrastive training loop for agent embeddings with triplet and InfoNCE losses, used to learn affinity-aware representations that downstream MoE routing and reasoning-bank retrieval consume.

nodes.name: adapter_integration.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests exercising LoRA adapter swapping and MicroLoRA per-request adaptation against the serving engine to validate end-to-end adapter lifecycle.

nodes.name: perplexity_baselines.json
nodes.summary: Reference perplexity, repetition, coherence, speed, and memory baselines for quantized model regression tests, gating ruvllm releases against quality drift.

nodes.name: pi_quant_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Unit and property tests for pi-quant 2-bit and 3-bit kernels, validating pack/unpack round-trips and SIMD/scalar parity to guard the inference quantization layer.

nodes.name: ModelDef
nodes.summary: Defines a downloadable test model with HuggingFace coordinates and expected size for the example loader.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Entry point for the model-download example; parses CLI arguments and dispatches to the chosen download backend.

nodes.name: Gemma2Config
nodes.summary: Hyperparameter container for Gemma2 variants (2B/9B/27B) with hidden size, head count, sliding window, and soft-cap settings.

nodes.name: Gemma2Attention
nodes.summary: Multi-head attention block with sliding-window masking and logit soft-cap used in Gemma2 decoder layers.

nodes.name: Gemma2MLP
nodes.summary: Gated MLP feed-forward block with NEON-optimized linear layers for Gemma2 decoder.

nodes.name: Gemma2DecoderLayer
nodes.summary: One Gemma2 decoder layer combining attention, MLP, and pre/post normalization blocks.

nodes.name: Gemma2Model
nodes.summary: Top-level Gemma2 model that wires embeddings, stacked decoder layers, and the final LM head for the serving engine to consume.

nodes.name: ExportTensor
nodes.summary: Tensor descriptor used while exporting BitNet weights to GGUF.

nodes.name: GgufBitnetWriter
nodes.summary: Streams a complete BitNet T1.58 model to disk in GGUF format with header, metadata, and ternary tensor blocks.

nodes.name: BpeTokenizer
nodes.summary: Byte-pair encoding tokenizer for BitNet models supporting vocab loading, encode/decode, and special-token handling.

nodes.name: PretrainPipeline
nodes.summary: Top-level controller that runs the bootstrap, synthetic, reinforce, and consolidate phases and persists checkpoints between runs.

nodes.name: CurriculumScheduler
nodes.summary: Curriculum stage tracker that advances difficulty based on rolling quality averages from the pipeline.

nodes.name: QualityGate
nodes.summary: Per-phase quality gate that blocks promotion of low-quality training samples in the Claude-Flow pipeline.

nodes.name: WorkingMemory
nodes.summary: Per-task working-memory container holding scratchpad entries, attention weights, and tool-result cache with embedding-based search.

nodes.name: TaskContext
nodes.summary: Captures the lifecycle metadata of a single task tracked in working memory.

nodes.name: SweBenchLoader
nodes.summary: Loads, parses, filters, and samples SWE-bench tasks from JSONL or remote cache for evaluation runs.

nodes.name: SweBenchTask
nodes.summary: Single SWE-bench task instance with parsed problem statement, patch, and metadata.

nodes.name: ProgressBar
nodes.summary: Stateful terminal progress bar with ETA and speed display used during model downloads.

nodes.name: MultiProgress
nodes.summary: Coordinates several concurrent progress bars when downloading multi-shard model files.

nodes.name: rms_norm_neon
nodes.summary: NEON SIMD RMSNorm entrypoint that dispatches to the inner kernel after safety checks.

nodes.name: layer_norm_neon
nodes.summary: NEON SIMD LayerNorm entrypoint with welford-style accumulators.

nodes.name: batched_rms_norm_neon
nodes.summary: Batched NEON RMSNorm operating over a [batch, hidden] slice for transformer micro-batches.

nodes.name: ExpertAffinity
nodes.summary: Tracks per-expert affinity scores with SIMD decay, top-k selection, and least-used identification for MoE routing decisions.

nodes.name: AffinityConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration record for affinity decay rate, history window, and load-balance thresholds.

nodes.name: PagedAttention
nodes.summary: Paged attention executor that performs KV lookups across a non-contiguous page table during the attention forward pass.

nodes.name: PageTable
nodes.summary: Block-allocator-backed page table mapping logical KV positions to physical memory blocks.

nodes.name: PagedAttentionConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration record for block size, allocation strategy, and pool sizing used by PagedAttention.

nodes.name: QatTrainer
nodes.summary: Quantization-Aware Training loop orchestrator handling calibration, per-step training, epoch reporting, and checkpoint resume.

nodes.name: TrainingPhase
nodes.summary: Enumerates the high-level phases (calibration, warmup, main, distillation) the QAT trainer walks through.

nodes.name: PiQuantizer
nodes.summary: Pi-quant quantizer offering scalar, block 3-bit, and block 2-bit kernels with adaptive alpha calibration and runtime SIMD dispatch.

nodes.name: Pi3BitBlock
nodes.summary: Packed 3-bit weight block with pack/unpack helpers used as the on-disk and in-memory representation for 3-bit pi-quant.

nodes.name: Pi2BitBlock
nodes.summary: Packed 2-bit weight block representation paired with NEON/AVX2 codecs for the most aggressive pi-quant tier.

nodes.name: PatternStore
nodes.summary: In-memory pattern store keyed by category supporting cosine-similarity search, pruning, and merging of reasoning patterns.

nodes.name: Pattern
nodes.summary: A single reasoning pattern derived from trajectories or lessons with similarity, usage, and quality bookkeeping.

nodes.name: ServingEngine
nodes.summary: Central LLM serving runtime that owns the model, draft model, scheduler, and async run loop and exposes blocking + streaming inference APIs.

nodes.name: ServingEngineConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration record for serving concurrency, coalescing, timeouts, streaming, and speculative-decoding toggles.

nodes.name: GenerationResult
nodes.summary: Completed generation payload returned to clients with tokens, timing, and finish-reason metadata.

nodes.name: ServingMetrics
nodes.summary: Aggregated serving-engine metrics covering throughput, latency, cache utilization, and uptime.

nodes.name: run_iteration
nodes.summary: One scheduler tick: pulls ready requests, runs token generation (with optional speculation), handles completions, and updates metrics.

nodes.name: generate_with_speculation
nodes.summary: Speculative decoding fast path that drafts multiple tokens with a small model and verifies them against the main model in a single forward.

nodes.name: stream
nodes.summary: Returns an async stream of generated tokens for a request, enabling SSE/server-push style serving.

nodes.name: submit_async
nodes.summary: Async submission API that registers a request and returns a future resolving to the completed generation result.

nodes.name: SpeculativeDecoder
nodes.summary: Coordinates draft model generation, target verification, and adaptive lookahead to deliver speculative-decoding speedups for the serving engine.

nodes.name: SpeculationTree
nodes.summary: Tree-of-thoughts speculation structure used to evaluate multiple draft paths in parallel before verification.

nodes.name: AtomicSpeculativeStats
nodes.summary: Atomic counters tracking accepted/rejected speculative tokens across concurrent serving requests.

nodes.name: softmax_neon_optimized
nodes.summary: NEON-vectorized softmax used by the sampling step of speculative verification.

nodes.name: top_p_filter

nodes.name: e2e_bench.rs
nodes.summary: End-to-end Criterion benchmarks for LLM serving covering single/multi-layer forward passes, KV-cache ops, decode throughput, prefill latency, and tokens-per-second estimation across Llama2/Llama3/Mistral configurations.
nodes.languageNotes: Criterion-based Rust microbenchmarks with model-sized configurations to estimate realistic serving throughput.

nodes.name: ModelConfig
nodes.summary: Holds reference dimensions for common open-source models (Llama2-7B/13B, Llama3-8B, Mistral-7B) used to parameterize benchmarks.

nodes.name: TransformerLayer
nodes.summary: Bench-only transformer-layer shim that performs a single-token forward pass using simple GEMV, RoPE, and attention helpers to estimate serving cost.

nodes.name: KvCache
nodes.summary: Simple growing KV cache used by the benchmark transformer layer to measure cache-append and memory growth patterns.

nodes.name: forward_single_token
nodes.summary: Runs one decode step (Q/K/V projection, RoPE, attention, output, FFN, residual) to model per-token serving latency for the benchmark suite.

nodes.name: bench_decode_throughput
nodes.summary: Measures tokens-per-second decode throughput across model sizes — a primary LLM-serving SLO.

nodes.name: bench_prefill_latency
nodes.summary: Measures prefill latency for variable prompt lengths, modeling the time-to-first-token cost of LLM serving.

nodes.name: bench_kv_cache_operations
nodes.summary: Times KV-cache append and read operations at varying context lengths to characterize memory traffic during serving.

nodes.name: bench_inference_components
nodes.summary: Micro-benchmarks individual inference primitives (GEMV, RoPE, attention) to attribute serving cost to specific kernels.

nodes.name: turbo_quant_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmarks for the TurboQuant compressed-vector subsystem covering compress/decompress throughput, inner-product accuracy, cache tiers, KV-cache, embedding store, and dimension scaling — sized to LLM-serving memory budgets.

nodes.name: bench_compress_throughput
nodes.summary: Times TurboQuant vector compression at varying bit widths to characterize the encode-side cost of serving-time quantization.

nodes.name: bench_kv_cache
nodes.summary: Benchmarks the TurboQuant-backed KV cache path used during serving — push/get/decompress at realistic context lengths.

nodes.name: bench_embedding_store
nodes.summary: Times compressed-embedding-store build/get/search to measure retrieval-augmented serving footprint.

nodes.name: bench_memory_efficiency
nodes.summary: Reports memory compression ratios across TurboQuant bit widths versus fp32 baselines for capacity-planning the serving footprint.

nodes.name: bench_dimension_scaling
nodes.summary: Sweeps embedding dimension to characterize how compressed inner-product cost scales with the model hidden size used at serving time.

nodes.name: ruvltra_small.json
nodes.summary: Model card / architecture descriptor for the RuvLTRA-Small 0.5B LLM (Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct base), declaring hidden size, attention heads, RoPE theta, GQA layout, and SoNA/ANE optimization flags consumed by the serving runtime.

nodes.name: TEST_COVERAGE.md
nodes.summary: Test-coverage report for the PT-BitNet b1.58 quantizer (ADR-017 Phase 0): 61 tests across 8 categories covering ternary packing, absmean quantization, dequantization, tensors, layer filtering, and edge cases used to validate serving-grade compressed weights.

nodes.name: ternary_tensor.rs

nodes.name: matmul_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark suite for ruvllm's matrix-multiplication kernels (GEMV, GEMM, batched GEMM, GEMM-NT, dot product) including NEON SIMD versus scalar fallbacks across realistic LLM projection sizes. Used to measure the throughput of attention and FFN linear layers driving inference performance.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses inline aarch64 NEON intrinsics with #[target_feature(enable = "neon")] for hand-tuned GEMV/GEMM paths.

nodes.name: GITHUB_ISSUE_SOTA_FEATURES.md
nodes.summary: GitHub issue draft proposing SOTA features (structured JSON output, speculative decoding, prefix caching, etc.) for production agentic LLM workflows served by ruvllm. Describes motivation, API sketches, and rollout plan for closing the gap with vLLM/TGI.

nodes.name: autodetect.rs
nodes.summary: Runtime hardware introspection layer that fingerprints platform, architecture, CPU features (AVX2/NEON/F16C), GPU/Metal/CUDA/WebGPU presence, core topology, ANE, and memory, then recommends optimal compute backend, batch size, quantization, and inference config for ruvllm.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses cfg(target_arch) feature flags and runtime CPUID/sysctl probes to keep the binary portable across macOS Apple Silicon, Linux x86_64, and Windows.

nodes.name: dequantize.rs
nodes.summary: Reference dequantizer for BitNet b1.58 ternary tensors, producing FP32 reconstructions and measuring quantization error. Used to validate the BitNet TL1 packed-weight format that powers ruvllm's 1.58-bit inference path.

nodes.name: tl1_avx2.rs
nodes.summary: AVX2 GEMV kernel for BitNet TL1 packed ternary weights, including i16 activation quantization, packed-index unpacking, and a scalar fallback. Provides the x86 hot path for 1.58-bit matrix-vector multiplies during inference.
nodes.languageNotes: Gated on cfg(target_arch = "x86_64") with #[target_feature(enable = "avx2")] for fine-grained dispatch.

nodes.name: hooks_integration.rs
nodes.summary: Implements ruvllm's Claude-Flow hook surface (pre/post task, pre/post edit, session lifecycle) that routes agent work between ruvllm and external agent-booster backends and records trajectories for Sona learning loops.

nodes.name: episodic_memory.rs
nodes.summary: Long-horizon episodic memory store for LLM context — stores compressed embedding+text episodes with metadata, supports similarity search, filtered retrieval, age-based compression, and capacity enforcement to augment generation with retrieved past episodes.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Module barrel for ruvllm's evaluation harness — re-exports correctness, diff-quality, economics, metrics, real-harness, report, and SWE-bench submodules used to score generations from inference runs.

nodes.name: download.rs
nodes.summary: Model-hub downloader that validates URLs and repo IDs, canonicalizes destination paths to guard against traversal, then fetches model weights via curl/wget with checksum verification and human-readable progress reporting.

nodes.name: attention.rs
nodes.summary: Core attention kernel library for ruvllm — Flash-Attention v2 (NEON and scalar), paged/multi-query/grouped-query/multi-head attention, KV-cache management with paged blocks, and tiled batched score plus softmax routines. The hot path that dominates LLM decoding latency.
nodes.languageNotes: Mixes aarch64 NEON intrinsics with scalar fallbacks and Rayon parallelism; PagedKvCache holds block-based K/V buffers for vLLM-style serving.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Public surface for ruvllm's LoRA adapter system — wraps RuvLtraAdapters registry, LoraConfig and its builder, AdapterMetadata, and delegates to merge/trainer/micro_lora submodules for per-domain adapter swapping at inference time.

nodes.name: pipelines.rs
nodes.summary: Apple Metal compute-pipeline cache and shader generator — compiles GEMM/attention MSL shaders on demand, caches pipeline objects keyed by config, and reports available Metal optimizations (simdgroup matrices, fp16, etc.) for Apple Silicon inference.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel for ruvllm's first-party model definitions, re-exporting the ruvltra and ruvltra_medium architectures used as default backends when no external model is loaded.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Barrel for the runtime optimization subsystem (metrics, realtime tuner, Sona-LLM feedback) that adjusts inference parameters under load based on live telemetry.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Quantization-Aware Training (QAT) module surface — wires calibration, config, differentiable quantizers, distillation, LoRA-QAT, reasoning-loss, straight-through estimators, and the training loop used to fine-tune low-bit checkpoints before serving.

nodes.name: hadamard.rs
nodes.summary: Hadamard rotation transform (QuaRot/SpinQuant style) for outlier-suppression prior to weight/activation quantization — implements scalar, NEON, and AVX2 butterfly kernels, sign application, normalization, and batch forward/inverse passes.

nodes.name: consolidation.rs
nodes.summary: Reasoning-pattern consolidator implementing Fisher-information-weighted EWC-style merging — collapses similar trajectory patterns, computes regularization losses, and adapts lambda to avoid catastrophic forgetting in ruvllm's reasoning bank.

nodes.name: reflective_agent.rs
nodes.summary: Reflexion-style reflective agent wrapper that re-executes an LLM task with structured self-critique on failure — manages reflection strategy, confidence scoring, error recovery, and multi-perspective retries while feeding outcomes back to the reasoning bank.

nodes.name: integration.rs
nodes.summary: Sona-LLM integration adapter — records inference trajectories, runs instant/background/deep self-improvement loops, computes pseudo-gradients, exposes routing recommendations, and exports learned policy back into the witness-log/policy-store.

nodes.name: tokenizer.rs
nodes.summary: Tokenizer and chat-template layer — detects format from model id (Llama3, Llama2, Mistral, ChatML, Phi, Gemma, custom), formats prompts accordingly, and provides a streaming decode buffer for incremental detokenization during generation.

nodes.name: types.rs
nodes.summary: Shared type vocabulary for ruvllm — ModelSize, Precision, AllocationType and related enums used throughout the inference engine to describe model footprints, dtype choices, and KV-cache placement.

nodes.name: e2e_integration.rs
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration test suite covering full inference pipeline, session lifecycle, KV-cache eviction and two-tier storage, paged attention, policy/witness log, Sona learning loops, and quantization/device variants for ruvllm.

nodes.name: mistral_backend_test.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests for ruvllm's mistral.rs backend wiring — verifies model loading, generation, streaming, embeddings, X-LoRA manager routing, ISQ in-situ quantization, paged-attention config, and serialization round-trips.

nodes.name: speculative_integration.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests for ruvllm's speculative-decoding path — covers config defaults, atomic stats, speculation tree construction and traversal, verification, softmax/top-k/top-p filters, and realistic multi-step speculation scenarios.

nodes.name: select_compute_backend_for_model
nodes.summary: Chooses the best compute backend (Metal, CUDA, CPU+AVX2/NEON, WebGPU) for a given model size using detected GPU and CPU capabilities.

nodes.name: optimal_quantization
nodes.summary: Recommends an optimal quantization scheme (BitNet, INT4, FP8, FP16) based on detected hardware features and memory budget.

nodes.name: detect_ane_tops
nodes.summary: Estimates Apple Neural Engine TOPS by interrogating the chip model (M1/M2/M3 Ultra/Pro/Max) on macOS.

nodes.name: summary
nodes.summary: Formats a human-readable multi-section summary of detected system capabilities used in startup logs and diagnostics.

nodes.name: SystemCapabilities
nodes.summary: Aggregate descriptor for the host's hardware capabilities — platform, architecture, CPU features, GPU, cores, ANE, and memory — feeding all backend/quantization decisions.

nodes.name: InferenceConfig
nodes.summary: Recommended inference configuration (backend, dtype, block size, max sequence length, batch size) derived from SystemCapabilities.

nodes.name: CpuFeatures
nodes.summary: Detected CPU SIMD/extension flags (AVX2, AVX-512, NEON, F16C) and the recommended SIMD width for the host.

nodes.name: GpuCapabilities
nodes.summary: Detected GPU presence and capabilities (Metal, CUDA, WebGPU) used when selecting the inference backend.

nodes.name: CoreInfo
nodes.summary: Logical/physical core topology including P/E core split on Apple silicon and hybrid x86 layouts.

nodes.name: dequantize_bitnet_t158
nodes.summary: Dequantizes a BitNet b1.58 ternary tensor into FP32 by unpacking ternary indices and applying the per-tensor scale.

nodes.name: dequantize_bitnet_block
nodes.summary: Dequantizes a single packed BitNet block, the inner step of full-tensor dequantization.

nodes.name: tl1_gemv_avx2
nodes.summary: AVX2-accelerated GEMV for BitNet TL1 packed ternary weights — unpacks indices and accumulates against int16 activations.

nodes.name: tl1_gemv
nodes.summary: Dispatch entry point that selects the AVX2 kernel when available, otherwise falls back to the scalar TL1 GEMV.

nodes.name: HooksIntegration
nodes.summary: Central Claude-Flow hooks coordinator — owns config, router, trajectory tracker, and embeddings; exposes pre/post task, edit, and session callbacks used by external orchestrators.

nodes.name: pre_task
nodes.summary: Pre-task hook that classifies the agent, builds a routing decision (ruvllm vs agent-booster), and opens a trajectory for later attribution.

nodes.name: post_task
nodes.summary: Post-task hook that closes the trajectory with outcome metrics and feeds the result back to the reasoning bank and routing policy.

nodes.name: route_task
nodes.summary: Routes a task between ruvllm and external backends based on embedding similarity and configured agent rules.

nodes.name: EpisodicMemory
nodes.summary: Append-and-search episodic memory store keyed by embedding; supports filtered retrieval, compression of old episodes, and bounded capacity.

nodes.name: MemoryCompressor
nodes.summary: Compresses episode text and embeddings into a compact representation, with helpers to extract recurring patterns for the reasoning bank.

nodes.name: store_episode
nodes.summary: Appends a new episode with embedding/text/metadata, enforces capacity, and optionally compresses on insert.

nodes.name: search_similar
nodes.summary: k-NN cosine similarity search across stored episode embeddings.

nodes.name: ModelDownloader
nodes.summary: Top-level downloader orchestrating URL/path validation, curl/wget fetch, progress reporting, and checksum verification for model artifacts.

nodes.name: ChecksumVerifier
nodes.summary: Computes and verifies SHA-256 (and friends) over downloaded files to confirm integrity before models are loaded.

nodes.name: validate_url
nodes.summary: Validates that a download URL is HTTPS and points at an allow-listed host before fetching weights.

nodes.name: validate_and_canonicalize_path
nodes.summary: Canonicalizes a destination path and rejects traversal-style segments to prevent overwriting files outside the model cache.

nodes.name: download_with_curl
nodes.summary: Shells out to curl with progress parsing to fetch a remote model artifact.

nodes.name: PagedKvCache
nodes.summary: Block-paged KV cache (vLLM-style) storing keys and values in fixed-size pages with append-only semantics and capacity bookkeeping.

nodes.name: AttentionScratch
nodes.summary: Reusable scratch buffers (running max, running sum, output accumulator) for online softmax inside Flash-Attention.

nodes.name: flash_attention_v2
nodes.summary: Top-level Flash-Attention v2 entry that picks NEON or scalar based on capabilities and dispatches to the appropriate implementation.

nodes.name: flash_attention_v2_neon_impl
nodes.summary: Inner NEON-vectorized Flash-Attention v2 kernel — tiles Q over K/V blocks and accumulates with online softmax.

nodes.name: paged_attention_neon
nodes.summary: Paged attention NEON kernel that consumes a PagedKvCache block table during decoding.

nodes.name: grouped_query_attention_parallel
nodes.summary: Rayon-parallel grouped-query attention used by Llama-style models that share KV heads across query heads.

nodes.name: softmax_neon
nodes.summary: NEON-vectorized numerically-stable softmax used by the attention scores stage.

nodes.name: RuvLtraAdapters
nodes.summary: Registry of LoRA adapters indexed by domain — supports lookup, swapping, and exporting micro-LoRA config snapshots for inference.

nodes.name: LoraConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration for a single LoRA adapter — rank, alpha, target modules, and conversion into the runtime micro-LoRA layout.

nodes.name: LoraConfigBuilder
nodes.summary: Fluent builder for LoraConfig used by callers configuring adapter loads.

nodes.name: MetalPipelines
nodes.summary: Owns the Metal device and the compiled-pipeline cache; exposes available optimizations and on-demand pipeline creation.

nodes.name: generate_gemm_shader
nodes.summary: Generates Metal Shading Language source for a GEMM kernel parameterized by tile size and dtype.

nodes.name: get_or_compile
nodes.summary: Returns a cached Metal pipeline for the given config, compiling it on cache miss.

nodes.name: HadamardTransform
nodes.summary: Hadamard rotation matrix with cached signs and scale, plus forward/inverse in-place transforms used to suppress outliers prior to weight or activation quantization.

nodes.name: hadamard_butterfly_neon
nodes.summary: NEON-vectorized in-place Hadamard butterfly that does add/sub passes across power-of-two strides.

nodes.name: hadamard_butterfly_avx2
nodes.summary: AVX2 counterpart to the NEON Hadamard butterfly, used on x86_64 hosts.

nodes.name: hadamard_batch_transform
nodes.summary: Applies the Hadamard transform across a batch of vectors in parallel for activation rotation prior to quantization.

nodes.name: PatternConsolidator
nodes.summary: EWC-style consolidator that merges similar reasoning patterns under Fisher-weighted regularization to avoid catastrophic forgetting.

nodes.name: FisherInformation
nodes.summary: Running Fisher-information estimator updated from gradient-like signals, used to weight pattern parameters during consolidation.

nodes.name: consolidate_patterns
nodes.summary: Main entry point that finds mergeable patterns, merges them, and applies Fisher-regularized constraints to the resulting store.

nodes.name: consolidate_fisher
nodes.summary: Applies Fisher-information weighting to constrain updates so high-importance pattern parameters are preserved.

nodes.name: Reflection
nodes.summary: Structured record of a reflection attempt — outcome, confidence, narrative, and any error-recovery actions taken.

nodes.name: ExecutionResult
nodes.summary: Outcome of an agent step (success, recovered, or failure) with attached metrics consumed by the learning loops.

nodes.name: BaseAgent
nodes.summary: Reflexion-style wrapper around an underlying task executor that adds self-critique, multi-perspective retries, and confidence scoring.

nodes.name: execute_with_reflection
nodes.summary: Runs a task, evaluates the result, and re-attempts with reflection-derived context until success criteria, max attempts, or budget are reached.

nodes.name: generate_reflection
nodes.summary: Produces a structured reflection over a failed/recovered execution, including confidence and proposed recovery actions.

nodes.name: retry_with_context
nodes.summary: Replays the task augmented with reflection context (errors, lessons learned) to steer the LLM toward a corrected output.

nodes.name: SonaIntegration
nodes.summary: Adapter that records inference trajectories, drives Sona's instant/background/deep learning loops, and exports learned routing policy back to the policy store and witness log.

nodes.name: record_trajectory
nodes.summary: Records an inference trajectory (prompt, response, reward) for later use by Sona's learning loops.

nodes.name: trigger_background_loop
nodes.summary: Launches Sona's background self-improvement loop that consumes accumulated trajectories and updates routing/policy parameters.

nodes.name: get_routing_recommendation
nodes.summary: Returns the current Sona-derived routing recommendation for an incoming request given its features.

nodes.name: export_to_policy_store
nodes.summary: Persists the latest Sona-learned routing policy into the shared policy store for downstream consumers.

nodes.name: ChatTemplate
nodes.summary: Chat-template router that detects and renders the correct prompt format (Llama3, Llama2, Mistral, ChatML, Phi, Gemma, custom) per model family.

nodes.name: StreamingDecodeBuffer
nodes.summary: Stateful byte buffer that converts streamed token ids into UTF-8 text safely across multi-byte boundaries.

nodes.name: detect_from_model_id
nodes.summary: Heuristically selects the correct chat template from the model identifier string.

nodes.name: format_llama3
nodes.summary: Formats a chat conversation using Llama-3's special-token chat template.

nodes.name: format_mistral
nodes.summary: Formats a chat conversation using Mistral's [INST]-style chat template.

nodes.name: ModelSize
nodes.summary: Coarse classification of model size buckets used to drive backend, batch, and quantization decisions.

nodes.name: Precision
nodes.summary: Numeric precision enum (FP32/FP16/BF16/FP8/INT8/INT4/BitNet) shared across kernels and config.

nodes.name: AllocationType
nodes.summary: KV-cache and tensor allocation strategy enum (e.g. unified-memory vs explicit-host vs explicit-device) used by the runtime.

nodes.name: metal_bench.rs
nodes.summary: Criterion benchmark suite exercising the Metal-accelerated LLM inference kernels — flash attention, GEMM, RMSNorm, RoPE, fused-norm-residual, SwiGLU — against a CPU baseline to validate Apple Silicon serving throughput.
nodes.languageNotes: Criterion benches comparing fused Metal kernels against CPU; key for proving 40+ tok/s claim on M-series.

nodes.name: GITHUB_ISSUE_V2.md
nodes.summary: Public-facing v2.0 announcement / GitHub issue describing RuvLLM as a Rust LLM inference engine for Apple Silicon (Metal + ANE + NEON) with feature highlights, model support, and serving performance targets.

nodes.name: candle_backend.rs
nodes.summary: Candle-based inference backend that loads transformer weights, drives token generation, and bridges Sona/tokenizer integration so the serving engine can execute models through the Candle runtime on CPU or Metal.
nodes.languageNotes: Large adapter file wrapping Candle tensors behind RuvLLM's backend trait; primary entry point for model forward passes.

nodes.name: eval.rs
nodes.summary: Evaluation gates for BitNet ternary-weight models — routing correctness, citation correctness, and refusal calibration — providing pass/fail signals that gate releases of quantized LLMs for serving.

nodes.name: tl1_kernel.rs
nodes.summary: BitNet TL1 ternary GEMV kernel — packs ternary weights, builds lookup tables, and ships both scalar and NEON SIMD implementations of the per-pair LUT matmul that powers low-bit LLM inference.
nodes.languageNotes: Hand-tuned ARM NEON intrinsics for 1.58-bit ternary matmul — the throughput hotspot for BitNet serving.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Claude Flow integration root that enumerates available agents, classifies task types, and re-exports the routing, classifier, and pretraining modules used to drive intelligent multi-model LLM serving.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Context-management barrel re-exporting agentic memory, episodic/working memory, semantic cache, and the Claude Flow bridge that together build per-session context for LLM serving requests.

nodes.name: real_harness.rs
nodes.summary: End-to-end evaluation harness that bootstraps an HNSW pattern store, drives real model inference with routing decisions, and scores generated patches for correctness/latency — the production benchmark loop for the LLM server.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Model-hub barrel exposing download, upload, registry, model-card, and progress submodules that handle pulling LLM weights into the local cache for serving.

nodes.name: matmul.rs
nodes.summary: Core matmul kernel collection — NEON GEMV/GEMM/GEMM-NT, F16 variants, batched GEMM, fused AXPBY, and Metal-backed GEMV dispatch — that powers every linear layer in the LLM serving forward pass.
nodes.languageNotes: Hot path for LLM inference; mixes scalar fallbacks, NEON intrinsics, F16 paths, and an `is_metal_gemv_available` runtime dispatch.

nodes.name: trainer.rs
nodes.summary: LoRA adapter training loop with dataset assembly, validation splits, checkpointing, and a synthetic example generator — used to fine-tune small adapters that personalize the served LLM without touching base weights.

nodes.name: attention.metal
nodes.summary: Flash Attention 2 Metal compute shader with tiled query/KV blocks and online softmax rescaling, targeting Apple M4 Pro simdgroup_matrix ops to deliver O(N)-memory attention for the LLM serving runtime.
nodes.languageNotes: Metal shader language; uses simdgroup_matrix on M4 Pro with 64x64 Q/KV tiles and 128-dim head cap.

nodes.name: ruvltra.rs
nodes.summary: Definition of the RuvLTRA transformer model — config presets (Qwen-0.5B/1.8B/tiny), attention/MLP/decoder-layer modules, ANE dispatch heuristics, and quantization-aware memory layouts — the native model architecture served by RuvLLM.

nodes.name: realtime.rs
nodes.summary: Realtime serving optimizer with adaptive batching, KV-cache pressure policies, token-budget allocation across in-flight requests, and speculative-decoding gating — drives throughput-vs-latency tradeoffs in the LLM serving loop.

nodes.name: reasoning_loss.rs
nodes.summary: Chain-of-thought distillation loss that compares teacher vs student reasoning trajectories with MSE/cosine metrics on step embeddings and final answers, used during QAT to preserve reasoning quality when quantizing the served LLM.

nodes.name: incoherence.rs
nodes.summary: Incoherence-processing layer applying randomized Hadamard transforms before quantization (and inverting after dequantization) to suppress outliers and reduce error in low-bit weights served by the LLM runtime.

nodes.name: distillation.rs
nodes.summary: Memory distiller that compresses raw agent trajectories into deduplicated key lessons (with embedding/content similarity merging) so the served LLM can retrieve compact, high-signal context instead of full episodic logs.

nodes.name: ruvector_integration.rs
nodes.summary: Top-level integration that fuses the RuVector unified index, reasoning bank, intelligence layer, and Claude Flow routing into one façade — the entry point the LLM server uses for context-aware retrieval, routing decisions, and online learning.

nodes.name: mod.rs
nodes.summary: Sona module barrel exposing the integration façade and RuvLTRA pretraining pipeline — the trajectory-recording/pretraining surface the LLM serving stack hooks into during training and online learning.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Training-module documentation covering fine-tuning datasets, contrastive learning with hard negatives, GRPO feedback loops, and the SOTA routing-accuracy numbers used to qualify served LLM checkpoints.

nodes.name: witness_log.rs
nodes.summary: Append-only witness log that records each LLM serving request's routing decision, latency breakdown, and quality signals to a durable async-flushed store, providing the audit trail and replay corpus for online learning.

nodes.name: e2e_integration_test.rs
nodes.summary: End-to-end integration test suite exercising GGUF loading, tokenization, generation, streaming, embeddings, and a mock serving engine — the top-level acceptance harness for the LLM server.

nodes.name: model_arch_integration.rs
nodes.summary: Integration tests covering Phi-3 and Gemma-2 architecture configs, RoPE scaling variants, chat-template formatting, and attention/KV-group invariants to guarantee multi-architecture model support in the LLM server.

nodes.name: ste_tests.rs
nodes.summary: Tests for the straight-through estimator (STE) used in quantization-aware training to preserve gradients through non-differentiable rounding when training the LLMs that RuvLLM serves.

nodes.name: ModelConfigInternal
nodes.summary: Internal model-config struct holding hidden/layer/vocab sizing used to instantiate transformer modules inside the Candle backend.

nodes.name: GateResult
nodes.summary: Pass/fail result for a single BitNet evaluation gate carrying score, threshold, and detail metrics consumed by the release pipeline.

nodes.name: EvalReport

nodes.name: EvalSuite

nodes.name: tl1_gemv_neon

nodes.name: generate_tl1_lut
nodes.summary: Builds the 9-entry lookup table for a ternary weight pair so the GEMV inner loop can replace multiplies with a table-indexed add/subtract.

nodes.name: tl1_gemv
nodes.summary: Public TL1 GEMV entry that quantizes activations and dispatches to the NEON or scalar implementation based on runtime capability.

nodes.name: ClaudeFlowAgent

nodes.name: ClaudeFlowTask

nodes.name: RealEvaluationHarness
nodes.summary: Stateful harness owning the inference backend, HNSW pattern store, and routing/learning loop that drives real-model evaluation runs against task suites.

nodes.name: RealInferenceConfig

nodes.name: bootstrap_hnsw_patterns

nodes.name: run_single_task
nodes.summary: Runs one evaluation task end-to-end: route, build context, generate, extract patch, score correctness — the unit-of-work for the real serving evaluation harness.

nodes.name: HubError
nodes.summary: Error enum for hub operations (download/upload/registry) with rich variants used by callers that fetch served LLM weights.

nodes.name: gemv_neon_impl
nodes.summary: Inner NEON-unrolled GEMV implementation operating on f32 weights and activations — the workhorse for LLM linear layers on CPU when Metal isn't used.

nodes.name: gemm_tile_12x4
nodes.summary: Highly unrolled 12x4 NEON GEMM tile kernel that accumulates into an output micro-tile, providing peak f32 throughput for batched matmuls in LLM serving.

nodes.name: gemm_nt_neon_impl
nodes.summary: NEON GEMM kernel where B is transposed (NT) — matches the layout of typical transformer weight matrices for efficient row-wise dot products during inference.

nodes.name: dot_product_neon
nodes.summary: Tight NEON dot-product reduction with multiple accumulators, used as a building block for attention scores and small projections.

nodes.name: batched_gemm_neon
nodes.summary: Batched NEON GEMM that loops over a batch dimension applying the tiled kernel — used for multi-head attention and continuous batching during serving.

nodes.name: gemv_metal_if_available
nodes.summary: Runtime-feature gate that routes GEMV to the Metal backend when present, otherwise falls back to the NEON CPU path — used during model dispatch in the serving loop.

nodes.name: TrainingExample

nodes.name: AdapterTrainingConfig

nodes.name: AdapterTrainer

nodes.name: SyntheticDataGenerator

nodes.summary: Main LoRA training loop: epochs over the dataset, per-step optimization, periodic validation, checkpoint persistence.

nodes.name: RuvLtraMLP

nodes.name: RuvLtraDecoderLayer
nodes.summary: Single transformer decoder layer combining attention, MLP, and residual norms — the stacked unit that makes up the served RuvLTRA model.

nodes.name: AneDispatcher
nodes.summary: Heuristic dispatcher choosing Apple Neural Engine vs GPU for each op based on batch/seq sizes, with op-level statistics for tuning.

nodes.name: forward
nodes.summary: Top-level forward pass — embed tokens, run all decoder layers with KV caches, project to logits — the function the serving loop calls per step.

nodes.name: RealtimeOptimizer
nodes.summary: Online optimizer for the serving loop combining adaptive batching, KV-cache eviction policy, token-budget allocation, and speculative-decoding gating to hit latency targets.

nodes.name: RealtimeConfig
nodes.summary: Configuration knobs (target latency, max batch, KV pressure thresholds, speculative settings) for the realtime serving optimizer.

nodes.summary: Speculative-decoding configuration — draft length, acceptance threshold, fallback policy — consumed by the optimizer when scoring whether to speculate.

nodes.name: ChainOfThoughtLoss
nodes.summary: Composite QAT loss measuring step-level, trajectory, and final-answer divergence between teacher and student reasoning to retain CoT quality when quantizing.

nodes.name: ReasoningStep
nodes.summary: Single CoT step with text, embedding, position and StepType tag — the unit the reasoning loss compares between teacher and student.

nodes.name: ReasoningConfig
nodes.summary: Weight presets balancing step/trajectory/answer terms — with `math_reasoning` and `code_generation` profiles — for the reasoning distillation loss.

nodes.name: IncoherenceTransform
nodes.summary: Caches randomized-Hadamard transforms and applies/inverts them around quantization to suppress outlier weights and lower the served model's quantization error.

nodes.name: IncoherenceConfig
nodes.summary: Quality/performance presets and seed control for the incoherence preprocessing applied before quantizing served-model weights.

nodes.name: apply_before_quantization
nodes.summary: Applies a randomized Hadamard rotation to weight tensors so outliers are smeared across coordinates before low-bit quantization.

nodes.name: restore_after_dequantization
nodes.summary: Inverse Hadamard rotation that restores original weight orientation after dequantization so the served model produces correct outputs.

nodes.name: MemoryDistiller
nodes.summary: Distills raw agent trajectories into deduplicated key lessons and summaries, compressing the reasoning bank so the served LLM retrieves compact, high-signal context.

nodes.name: KeyLesson

nodes.name: CompressedTrajectory
nodes.summary: Compact form of an agent trajectory with embedded summary statistics — stored after distillation to bound memory growth.

nodes.name: extract_key_lessons
nodes.summary: Pulls the top-N lessons out of a batch of trajectories using clustering and similarity scoring so only high-signal context reaches the served LLM.

nodes.name: generate_summary
nodes.summary: Builds a TrajectorySummary aggregating success rates, common patterns, and embeddings used as a quick-lookup digest for the LLM serving context.

nodes.name: RuvectorIntegration
nodes.summary: Façade that wires the unified index, intelligence layer, reasoning bank, and capabilities into one entry point used by the LLM serving stack.

nodes.name: UnifiedIndex
nodes.summary: HNSW-backed unified vector index with metadata, batched add, and attention-aware search — the retrieval primitive that feeds context into the served LLM.

nodes.name: IntelligenceLayer
nodes.summary: Routing intelligence that selects models/agents per task, learns from outcomes, and triggers background/deep learning runs to improve future serving decisions.

nodes.name: VectorMetadata

nodes.name: WritebackQueue

nodes.name: AsyncWriteConfig

tour.title: Slice 5a Orientation: ruvllm in the RuVector Stack
tour.description: Start with the ruvllm crate README to ground yourself in what this slice delivers — a Rust LLM inference engine that sits on top of slices 1-4 (vectors, sparsifiers, mincut, attention primitives) and exposes serving, training, and continual-learning APIs to the rest of RuVector. The CHANGELOG and Cargo.toml round out the picture by showing how the crate's feature flags gate Candle, Mistral.rs, CoreML, Metal, BitNet, and Claude Flow backends behind opt-in builds. Read these before any code so the module names you encounter later map to a published feature surface, not just files on disk.

tour.title: ruvllm Crate Root: RuvLLMEngine Facade
tour.description: lib.rs is the entry point the BFS analysis identified as the top code node — it re-exports every submodule and defines RuvLLMEngine/RuvLLMConfig, the facade that wires sessions, policy store, witness log, and Sona together. error.rs is the single most depended-on file in slice 5a (fan-in 82): nearly every module returns RuvLLMError, so understanding its variants tells you what can go wrong across the stack. types.rs holds the shared GenerateRequest/Response/Token shapes that flow through scheduling, generation, and streaming.
tour.languageLesson: Rust crates use `lib.rs` as the implicit module root. `pub use` re-exports flatten the public API so consumers can write `ruvllm::RuvLLMEngine` instead of `ruvllm::engine::RuvLLMEngine`. The pattern is called a facade or barrel module.

tour.title: Serving Engine: Continuous Batching & Paged KV Cache
tour.description: This is the runtime heart of ruvllm. engine.rs submits, schedules, generates, streams, and times out inference requests; scheduler.rs runs the continuous-batching loop with priority queues, prefill/decode interleaving, preemption, and eviction; kv_cache_manager.rs allocates block-aligned cache slots per request with swap-in/out for long-context tenants; batch.rs plans iterations against a token budget. Together they implement the same paged-attention + continuous-batching design popularized by vLLM, but in Rust and integrated with the rest of RuVector. speculative.rs adds draft/verify decoding with tree-of-thoughts speculation and NEON-optimized sampling on top.

tour.title: Backends: Candle / Mistral.rs / CoreML / Hybrid Dispatch
tour.description: The serving engine doesn't run models directly — it dispatches through the LlmBackend trait defined in backends/mod.rs (the second-most depended-on node after error.rs). candle_backend.rs is the default pure-Rust path using Candle; mistral_backend.rs adds production-grade Mistral.rs with PagedAttention + X-LoRA + in-situ quantization; coreml_backend.rs targets Apple's Neural Engine via .mlpackage. hybrid_pipeline.rs is the most interesting one: it routes each transformer op (flash attention, MLP, RMSNorm, RoPE) to whichever accelerator on Apple Silicon (CPU, Metal, or ANE) wins per-layer, and records per-accelerator timings. autodetect.rs + capabilities.rs decide which backend the engine picks at startup.
tour.languageLesson: Rust traits as compile-time interfaces let you implement `LlmBackend` for radically different runtimes (Candle, Mistral.rs, CoreML) and select between them via a `Box<dyn LlmBackend>`. Feature flags in Cargo.toml conditionally compile each backend so consumers only pay for what they use.

tour.title: Models: RuvLtra Architectures + GGUF Loading
tour.description: ruvltra.rs and ruvltra_medium.rs define the actual decoder-only transformer architectures (Qwen-0.5B/1.8B presets plus a medium GQA variant with RoPE and Sona hooks). They are designed to be loaded from GGUF — the quantization-friendly file format pioneered by llama.cpp. gguf/parser.rs streams headers, metadata, and tensor descriptors; gguf/loader.rs mmaps the file, filters layers, and translates upstream tensor names to ruvllm's internal naming via TensorNameMapper. The ruvltra_small.json descriptor seeds the registry with hardware requirements.

tour.title: Metal GPU: Shaders & Host Pipelines
tour.description: On Apple Silicon, the hybrid pipeline from Step 4 drops into hand-written Metal Shading Language for the hottest kernels. metal/mod.rs declares the kernel parameter structs and device-info gating; pipelines.rs compiles MSL shaders on demand and caches MTLComputePipelineState objects keyed by config; operations.rs / buffers.rs / context.rs wrap dispatch. The .metal files implement Flash Attention 2 with online softmax rescaling against simdgroup_matrix on M-series chips, fused GEMM/GEMV, RoPE, and norm kernels. This is where ruvllm earns its tokens-per-second on Mac.
tour.languageLesson: Metal Shading Language (MSL) is a C++14-based GPU language. `simdgroup_matrix<T, 8, 8>` exposes Apple's tensor-core-equivalent matrix instructions — Flash Attention 2 tiles Q/K/V into these blocks and runs an online softmax rescaling pass to avoid materializing the full N×N attention matrix.

tour.title: Attention Kernels: Flash Attention 2, Paged Attention, RoPE, Norms
tour.description: Cross-platform CPU fallbacks live in kernels/. attention.rs implements Flash Attention v2 with NEON and scalar paths plus multi-query/grouped-query variants; paged_attention.rs (depth-1 in the BFS, fan-in 6) gives the serving layer the non-contiguous KV memory it needs for high concurrency. rope.rs is NEON-accelerated rotary embeddings with NTK-aware scaling for LLaMA-2/3 and Mistral; norm.rs holds the RMSNorm/LayerNorm hot paths every layer hits. activations.rs and matmul.rs round out the kernel surface so non-Apple hardware still gets reasonable throughput.

tour.title: Quantization Stack I: BitNet Ternary
tour.description: BitNet b1.58 is the headline compression scheme — weights live in {-1, 0, +1} so multiplication becomes addition. bitnet/mod.rs wires together the subsystem and is a high-fan-in cluster anchor; ternary_tensor.rs packs ternary values into 2-bit fields with per-tensor scale; tl1_avx2.rs and tl1_wasm.rs are the x86 and browser GEMV hot paths; tl1_kernel.rs is the scalar reference; dequantize.rs and gguf_export.rs let you round-trip BitNet weights through GGUF. The cluster analysis flagged these files as one of the most tightly coupled subsystems in slice 5a.
tour.languageLesson: BitNet b1.58 stores each weight as ~1.58 bits (log2 of 3 states). Two ternary values pack into one byte's lower 4 bits. GEMV becomes a signed sum-and-subtract over int8 activations — no floating-point multiplies on the weight matrix, so AVX2's `pmaddubsw` becomes the workhorse.

tour.title: Quantization Stack II: TurboQuant, Hadamard, Pi-Quant, QAT
tour.description: Beyond BitNet, slice 5a ships a full post-training and training-time quantization toolkit. quantize/turbo_quant.rs is Hadamard-rotated scalar quantization with KV-cache and embedding-store front-ends (4-32× compression while preserving inner-product accuracy). hadamard.rs is the QuaRot/SpinQuant butterfly transform with scalar/NEON/AVX2 paths used to suppress activation outliers before quantization. pi_quant_simd.rs is the bit-packed PI-3/PI-2 codec across NEON/AVX2/AVX-512. qat/mod.rs anchors quantization-aware training; ste.rs implements straight-through estimators for non-differentiable quant ops; lora_qat.rs combines fake-quant with LoRA adapters so you can fine-tune a quantized model without dequantizing the base.

tour.title: Mixture of Experts: Precision Allocator & Router
tour.description: MoE inside ruvllm is unusual because each expert can run at a different precision. moe/precision_allocator.rs (ADR-092) assigns Q4_K_M / PiQ3 / PiQ2 tiers based on activation frequency — hot experts keep more bits, cold experts compress aggressively. router.rs picks top-k experts with cache-mask paging and per-request priorities; affinity.rs tracks expert-affinity scores with NEON/AVX2 decay; sram_mapper.rs maps active experts into fast SRAM tiles for serving. Together they let ruvllm serve large sparse models on memory-constrained devices.

tour.title: LoRA Adapters: Registry, Composition, Hot-Swap, Continual Learning
tour.description: ruvllm treats LoRA adapters as first-class runtime objects. lora/adapter.rs is the registry + composer that loads/unloads micro-LoRAs and composes them (sequential, weighted, task-routed) during multi-tenant serving. adapters/merge.rs implements average/weighted/SLERP/TIES/DARE merging for hot-swap without reloading the base model. adapter_manager.rs (BFS depth 1) enforces memory budgets for the adapter pool. training.rs adds an online training loop with EWC-regularized continual learning, gradient accumulation, and queued user feedback — so the served model can absorb corrections without catastrophic forgetting.

tour.title: Claude Flow & ReasoningBank: Trajectories, Patterns, EWC Consolidation
tour.description: This is ruvllm's continual-learning brain. claude_flow/mod.rs enumerates agent types and routes incoming tasks; agent_router.rs combines keyword caches with SONA-learned routing. reasoning_bank/trajectory.rs records every reasoning step (tokens, tool usage, embeddings, lessons); consolidation.rs implements Fisher-information-weighted EWC merging that collapses similar patterns and computes regularization losses. sona/mod.rs is the trajectory-recording + pretraining façade slice 5a hooks into. ruvector_integration.rs ties this brain back to the slice-3 unified index and slice-4 attention primitives.

tour.title: Reflection, Quality & Witness Logging
tour.description: Generations are wrapped in a quality pipeline before they ship. quality/coherence.rs checks semantic consistency and detects contradictions; diversity.rs computes lexical + embedding diversity and flags mode collapse. The Reflexion-style wrapper in reflection/reflective_agent.rs re-runs a task with structured self-critique on failure (cluster analysis identified reflective_agent + perspectives + confidence + claude_flow/mod as one of the tightest clusters in slice 5a). Every routed request lands in witness_log.rs — an append-only, async-flushed audit trail that records the routing decision, latency breakdown, and quality signals for offline analysis.

tour.title: Hub & Registry: Model Download + Policy Store
tour.description: Before serving, you need weights on disk and a policy to load them under. hub/registry.rs catalogs RuvLtra base models and adapters by size and quantization, filters by hardware requirements, and resolves Hugging Face download URLs. hub/download.rs validates URLs, canonicalizes destination paths to block directory traversal, and fetches weights via curl/wget with checksum verification. policy_store.rs persists quantization and router policies as vector-indexed entries backed by SONA — so runtime configuration is itself searchable. Together they form the safe ingress path for new models.

tour.title: Training: GRPO, MCP Tool-Use, Contrastive Embeddings
tour.description: Slice 5a doesn't just serve — it trains. training/grpo.rs implements Group Relative Policy Optimization: group samples, compute relative advantages with GAE, apply KL-regularized policy updates (the RL technique behind DeepSeek-R1). training/mcp_tools.rs ingests Model Context Protocol tool-call trajectories and builds GRPO samples with category-aware reward shaping for tool-use fine-tuning. training/contrastive.rs and real_trainer.rs run triplet + InfoNCE losses to learn agent embeddings that downstream MoE routing (Step 10) and ReasoningBank retrieval (Step 12) consume.

tour.title: Tests, Benches & Examples
tour.description: The verification surface ties everything back to measurable outcomes. tests/e2e_integration.rs exercises the full inference pipeline — session lifecycle, KV eviction, paged attention, policy + witness logging, Sona learning loop — and is the best single file to read after the tour. benches/serving_bench.rs compares sequential vs continuous-batching throughput so regressions in Step 3 are caught. examples/benchmark_model.rs is a runnable CLI that loads a model and emits tokens/s + percentile-latency JSON reports, and the fixture JSON files anchor perplexity baselines that pi_quant_tests and friends regress against.

tour.title: Capstone: How ruvllm Consumes Slices 1-4
tour.description: Pull it all together. ruvector_integration.rs is the seam where slice 5a meets the earlier slices: it fuses the slice-1/2/3 unified vector index, the slice-4 attention/mincut primitives, the ReasoningBank from Step 12, and the Claude Flow router into one façade that the LLM server depends on. paged_attention.rs and kernels/attention.rs are the direct consumers of slice-4 attention math. The Cargo.toml manifest, revisited now, shows exactly which slice-1-through-4 crates ruvllm depends on — making the architectural picture concrete: ruvllm is the inference-and-learning capstone that turns the previously-built primitives into a working agent runtime.
